tuning: E A D G B E
content:
[tab]     [ch]A[/ch]      [ch]D[/ch]      [ch]E[/ch]      [ch]E7[/ch]
e|---0------2------0------0-----|
B|---2------3------0------3-----|
G|---2------2------1------1-----|
D|---2------0------2------2-----|
A|---0-------------2------2-----|
E|-----------------0------0-----|[/tab]

Capo 3rd Fret

(In a moderate country half-time)

[Verse]
[tab][ch]A[/ch]                           [ch]D[/ch]             [ch]A[/ch]
Ev'ryone considered him the coward of the county.[/tab]
[tab]                                                     [ch]E[/ch]
He'd never stood one single time to prove the county wrong.[/tab]
[tab]    [ch]A[/ch]                         [ch]D[/ch]                     [ch]A[/ch]
His mama named him Tommy, the folks just called him yellow,[/tab]
[tab]                                       [ch]E[/ch]             [ch]A[/ch]
but something always told me they were reading Tommy wrong.[/tab]

[tab][ch]A[/ch]                                  [ch]D[/ch]             [ch]A[/ch]
He was only ten years old when his daddy died in prison.[/tab]
[tab]                                                [ch]E[/ch]
I looked after Tommy 'cause he was my brother's son.[/tab]
[tab]  [ch]A[/ch]                               [ch]D[/ch]               [ch]A[/ch]
I still recall the final words my brother said to Tommy:[/tab]
[tab]                           [ch]E[/ch]             [ch]A[/ch]
"Son, my life is over, but yours is just begun.[/tab]

[Chorus]
[tab][ch]A[/ch]                       [ch]D[/ch]                  [ch]A[/ch]
Promise me, son, not to do the things I've done.[/tab]
[tab][ch]D[/ch]              [ch]A[/ch]              [ch]E[/ch]
Walk away from trouble if you can.[/tab]
[tab][ch]A[/ch]                                [ch]D[/ch]              [ch]A[/ch]
It won't mean you're weak if you turn the other cheek.[/tab]
[tab]                  [ch]D[/ch]              [ch]E[/ch]
I hope you're old enough to understand:[/tab]
[tab]     [ch]E7[/ch]                              [ch]A[/ch]
Son, you don't have to fight to be a man."[/tab]

(Slide capo to 4th fret)

[Verse]
[tab][ch]A[/ch]                                [ch]D[/ch]                [ch]A[/ch]
There's someone for ev'ryone and Tommy's love was Becky.[/tab]
[tab]                                             [ch]E[/ch]
In her arms he didn't have to prove he was a man.[/tab]
[tab]    [ch]A[/ch]                            [ch]D[/ch]                [ch]A[/ch]
One day while he was workin' the Gatlin boys came callin'.[/tab]
[tab]                           [ch]E[/ch] (Spoken)  [ch]A[/ch]
They took turns at Becky.... There was three of them![/tab]

[tab][ch]A[/ch]                            [ch]D[/ch]             [ch]A[/ch]
Tommy opened up the door and saw his Becky cryin'.[/tab]
[tab]                                                          [ch]E[/ch]
The torn dress, the shattered look was more than he could stand.[/tab]
[tab]   [ch]A[/ch]                                    [ch]D[/ch]                [ch]A[/ch]
He reached above the fireplace and took down his daddy's picture.[/tab]
[tab]                                          [ch]E[/ch]                 [ch]A[/ch]
As his tears fell on his daddy's face, he heard these words again:[/tab]

[Chorus]
[tab][ch]A[/ch]                       [ch]D[/ch]                  [ch]A[/ch]
"Promise me, son, not to do the things I've done.[/tab]
[tab][ch]D[/ch]              [ch]A[/ch]              [ch]E[/ch]
Walk away from trouble if you can.[/tab]
[tab][ch]A[/ch]                                [ch]D[/ch]              [ch]A[/ch]
It won't mean you're weak if you turn the other cheek.[/tab]
[tab]                  [ch]D[/ch]              [ch]E[/ch]
I hope you're old enough to understand:[/tab]
[tab]     [ch]E7[/ch]                              [ch]A[/ch]
Son, you don't have to fight to be a man."[/tab]

(Slide capo to 5th fret)

[Verse]
[tab][ch]A[/ch]                                           [ch]D[/ch]               [ch]A[/ch]
The Gatlin boys just laughed at him when he walked into the barroom.[/tab]
[tab]                                                  [ch]E[/ch]
One of them got up and met him halfway 'cross the floor.[/tab]
[tab]     [ch]A[/ch]                                   [ch]D[/ch]                  [ch]A[/ch]
When Tommy turned around they said, "Hey look! ol' yellow's leavin'."[/tab]
[tab](Spoken)                             [ch]E[/ch]                 [ch]A[/ch]
But you coulda heard a pin drop when Tommy stopped and blocked the door.[/tab]

[tab][ch]A[/ch]                            [ch]D[/ch]             [ch]A[/ch]
Twenty years of crawlin' was bottled up inside him.[/tab]
[tab]                                                   [ch]E[/ch]
He wasn't holdin' nothin' back; he let 'em have it all.[/tab]
[tab]     [ch]A[/ch]                            [ch]D[/ch]              [ch]A[/ch]
When Tommy left the barroom not a Gatlin boy was standin'.[/tab]
[tab]                                       [ch]E[/ch]                    [ch]A[/ch]
He said, "This one's for Becky," as he watched the last one fall.[/tab]
(Spoken)
And I heard him say,

[Chorus]
[tab][ch]A[/ch]                            [ch]D[/ch]                 [ch]A[/ch]
"I promised you, Dad, not to do the things you done.[/tab]
[tab][ch]D[/ch]                [ch]A[/ch]             [ch]E[/ch]
I walk away from trouble when I can.[/tab]
[tab]    [ch]A[/ch]                                     [ch]D[/ch]              [ch]A[/ch]
Now please don't think I'm weak, I didn't turn the other cheek,[/tab]
[tab]                 [ch]D[/ch]             [ch]E[/ch]
and Papa, I sure hope you understand:[/tab]
[tab]    [ch]E7[/ch]                                  [ch]A[/ch]
Sometimes you gotta fight when you're a man."[/tab]


[tab][ch]A[/ch]                           [ch]D[/ch]             N.C.      [ch]A[/ch]
Ev'ryone considered him the coward of the county.[/tab]




        COWARD OF THE COUNTY
